title: Quantum-Fractal Logical Unified Field Theory
content:
# Quantum-Fractal Logical Unified Field Theory

Authors: Vidal Silvente, Enrique
Published: 2025-08-12T07:30:18Z
Subjects: Mathematical Sciences, Physics
DOI: doi:10.7910/DVN/034M7Z

This community hosts original research on the Quantum–Fractal Logical Unified Field Theory, a novel framework integrating quantum field theory, fractal geometry, logical computation, and number theory. The approach is centered around a self-similar, resonant structure of the vacuum field, where fundamental interactions, prime number distributions, and physical constants emerge as spectral invariants of a logical–fractal operator. Key topics include: Fractal Hilbert spaces and logical projections Resonant integral operators and their spectral modes Prime number prediction and the Riemann Hypothesis Logical coherence as a physical force Applications to cryptography, consciousness, and energy extraction The theory proposes a unifying field model governed by a recursive logical structure encoded through quantum-fractal operators, offering new insights into foundational problems in physics and mathematics.

Citation: Vidal Silvente, Enrique, 2025, "Quantum-Fractal Logical Unified Field Theory", https://doi.org/10.7910/DVN/034M7Z, Harvard Dataverse, V6
